query: Could you point me to research on binary classification systems that predict whether sentences within a context require clarification, particularly using the wikiHowToImprove dataset?

title.value: Asking Clarification Questions in Knowledge-Based Question Answering

recommendation_reason.value: The ability to ask clarification questions is essential for knowledge-based question answering (KBQA) systems, especially for handling ambiguous phenomena.

supported_research_question.value: given a question, identify whether clarification is needed

method.value: We provide representative baselines for these tasks

data_or_scenario.value: we construct a new clarification dataset, CLAQUA, with nearly 40K open-domain examples.

main_conclusion.value: Experiments show that the proposed model achieves better performance than strong baselines.

limitations.value: there still remain several unsolved problems, like reasonable automatic evaluation metrics for clarification question generation and powerful models for handling entity sparsity.

relevance_strength.value: The dataset supports three serial tasks: given a question, identify whether clarification is needed

title.value: Predicting the presence of inline citations in academic text using binary classification

recommendation_reason.value: The goal of this study was to determine what kind of accuracy we could reach in predicting whether or not a sentence should contain an inline citation using a simple binary classification model.

supported_research_question.value: The goal of this study was to determine what kind of accuracy we could reach in predicting whether or not a sentence should contain an inline citation using a simple binary classification model.

method.value: we fine-tuned SciBERT on both an imbalanced and a balanced dataset containing sentences with and without inline citations.

data_or_scenario.value: both an imbalanced and a balanced dataset containing sentences with and without inline citations

main_conclusion.value: We achieved an overall accuracy of over 0.92, suggesting that language patterns alone could be used to predict where inline citations should appear.

limitations.value: The goal of this study was to determine what kind of accuracy we could reach in predicting whether or not a sentence should contain an inline citation using a simple binary classification model.

relevance_strength.value: The goal of this study was to determine what kind of accuracy we could reach in predicting whether or not a sentence should contain an inline citation using a simple binary classification model.

title.value: UnImplicit Shared Task Report: Detecting Clarification Requirements in Instructional Text

recommendation_reason.value: The task requires computational models to predict whether a sentence contains aspects of meaning that are contextually unspecified and thus require clarification.

supported_research_question.value: Detecting Clarification Requirements in Instructional Text

method.value: The task requires computational models to predict whether a sentence contains aspects of meaning that are contextually unspecified and thus require clarification.

main_conclusion.value: the best scoring system achieved an accuracy of 68%.

limitations.value: Annotators and systems were provided with additional context, here shortened in brackets.

relevance_strength.value: detect whether a sentence from an instructional text requires clarification

matrix.title: Predicting the presence of inline citations in academic text using binary classification

cells.snippet: The goal of this study was to determine what kind of accuracy we could reach in predicting whether or not a sentence should contain an inline citation using a simple binary classification model.
To that end, we fine-tuned SciBERT on both an imbalanced and a balanced dataset containing sentences with and without inline citations.
The goal of this study was to determine what kind of accuracy we could reach in predicting whether or not a sentence should contain an inline citation using a simple binary classification model.
